pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **I feel for Devin, I really do, but when you draft Haiti in the fourth round, I mean... what conversation were you having with yourself?** Look, I get why people say "Ghost Pepper" had a plan, but it's not like Bosnia is going to scare a team like Canada, particularly when playing in Toronto. "Bubba G" might be in fourth place right now, but picking Canada as a Tier 3 team actually made some sense. Meanwhile, Devin's in third and hasn't got any points on the board, bless his heart. Now, I think it's safe to say that Gunner will take this one comfortably, given that Devin's line-up looks more like a dartboard guess than a strategic draft. My prediction? Canada's going to make Bosnia look like they're playing an entirely different sport.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's Canada Ties Ghost Pepper's Bosnia, Still Scores Zero
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner wonders if all matches have penalties for scoring.

pundit_takes.headline: The Backpass Assassin Tops Standings Despite Drafting Jordan
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden claims strategic advantage in losing more efficiently.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper's Spain Selection Yet To Spice Up Victory Column
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin reconsiders chili contest as training for managing.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Brazil Pick Fizzles In Fantasy Flop
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach blames condiment-inspired team spirit for zero points.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner, the favorite, already feeling heat from teenage chaos agent Rafe.
Rafe's draft blunders could fill a blooper reel, but he still ranks second.
Rafe took Devin to school with Mexico trouncing South Africa 2-0.
Sim underestimated Rafe's rookie upset; even chaos agents reject the script.
